text:
Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
// Consider TREE represented by level-order array above
// Perform inorder traversal (recursive)
INORDER(node)
  if node == NULL return
  INORDER(node.left)
  visit(node)
  INORDER(node.right)
```
What is the output sequence of INORDER on TREE?

Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
// Preorder traversal (recursive)
PREORDER(node)
  if node == NULL return
  visit(node)
  PREORDER(node.left)
  PREORDER(node.right)
```
What is the output sequence of PREORDER on TREE?

Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
// Postorder traversal (recursive)
POSTORDER(node)
  if node == NULL return
  POSTORDER(node.left)
  POSTORDER(node.right)
  visit(node)
```
What is the output sequence of POSTORDER on TREE?

Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
// Level-order (BFS) using queue
BFS(root)
  enq(root)
  while queue not empty
    node = deq(); visit(node)
    if node.left enq(node.left)
    if node.right enq(node.right)
```
What is the level-order output of TREE?

Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [4,2,6,1,3,5,7]
// Level-order (BFS) using queue
BFS(root)
  enq(root)
  while queue not empty
    node = deq(); visit(node)
    if node.left enq(node.left)
    if node.right enq(node.right)
```
What is the level-order output of TREE?

Pseudocode:
```
HEIGHT(node)
  if node == NULL return 0
  return 1 + max(HEIGHT(node.left), HEIGHT(node.right))
// TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
```
What is HEIGHT(TREE)?

Pseudocode:
```
HEIGHT(node)
  if node == NULL return 0
  return 1 + max(HEIGHT(node.left), HEIGHT(node.right))
// TREE = [1,2,3,null,5,null,7]
```
What is HEIGHT(TREE)?

Pseudocode:
```
COUNT-NODES(node)
  if node == NULL return 0
  return 1 + COUNT-NODES(node.left) + COUNT-NODES(node.right)
// TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
```
How many nodes does TREE have?

Pseudocode:
```
COUNT-LEAVES(node)
  if node == NULL return 0
  if node.left == NULL and node.right == NULL return 1
  return COUNT-LEAVES(node.left) + COUNT-LEAVES(node.right)
// TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
```
How many leaf nodes does TREE have?

```
INORDER-TRAVERSAL(node)
```

Is the inorder traversal of TREE sorted?

Pseudocode:
```
LCA(root,p,q)
  if root == NULL return NULL
  if root == p or root == q return root
  left = LCA(root.left,p,q)
  right = LCA(root.right,p,q)
  if left != NULL and right != NULL return root
  return (left != NULL) ? left : right
// TREE = [4,2,6,1,3,5,7]
```
What node value is LCA of 1 and 3?

Pseudocode:
```
LCA(root,p,q)
  if root == NULL return NULL
  if root == p or root == q return root
  left = LCA(root.left,p,q)
  right = LCA(root.right,p,q)
  if left != NULL and right != NULL return root
  return (left != NULL) ? left : right
// TREE = [4,2,6,1,3,5,7]
```
What node value is LCA of 1 and 7?

Pseudocode:
```
TO-CHAR-ARRAY(S)
  // returns array A[1..n] of characters
  n = length[S]
  for i = 1 to n
    A[i] = S[i]
  return A
```
What does TO-CHAR-ARRAY('code') return (format ['c','o','d','e'])?

Pseudocode:
```
CHAR-ARRAY-TO-STRING(A)
  // A[1..n] is a char array
  S = ""
  for i = 1 to length[A]
    S = S + A[i]
  return S
```
What is CHAR-ARRAY-TO-STRING(['h','e','l','p'])?

Pseudocode:
```
ROTATE-RIGHT(S,k)
  n = length[S]
  k = k mod n
  if k == 0 return S
  return S[n-k+1..n] + S[1..n-k]
```
What is ROTATE-RIGHT('abcd', 1)?

Pseudocode:
```
SHIFT-LEFT-K(S,k)
  n = length[S]
  k = k mod n
  return S[k+1..n] + S[1..k]
```
What is SHIFT-LEFT-K('abcdef', 2)?

Pseudocode:
```
SHIFT-RIGHT-K(S,k)
  n = length[S]
  k = k mod n
  return S[n-k+1..n] + S[1..n-k]
```
What is SHIFT-RIGHT-K('abcdef', 2)?

Pseudocode:
```
REVERSE-EACH-WORD(S)
  // reverse each word but keep words order
  i = 1
  result = ""
  while i <= length[S]
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] == ' ' do i = i + 1
    start = i
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] != ' ' do i = i + 1
    end = i - 1
    // append reversed word S[start..end]
    for j = end downto start
      result = result + S[j]
    if i <= length[S] then result = result + ' '
  return result
```
What is REVERSE-EACH-WORD('hello world')?

Pseudocode:
```
SMALLEST-LARGEST-WORD(S)
  // words separated by single spaces for simplicity
  minW = first word; maxW = first word
  for each word w in S
    if length[w] < length[minW] then minW = w
    if length[w] > length[maxW] then maxW = w
  return (minW, maxW)
```
For S = 'a bb ccc dddd' what does SMALLEST-LARGEST-WORD return (format 'min, max')?

Pseudocode:
```
COUNT-WORDS(S)
  count = 0
  i = 1
  while i <= length[S]
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] == ' ' do i = i + 1
    if i > length[S] then break
    count = count + 1
    while i <= length[S] and S[i] != ' ' do i = i + 1
  return count
```
What is COUNT-WORDS('Hello world from Cormen')?

Pseudocode:
```
MOST-FREQ-WORD(S)
  words = parse words from S
  freq = empty map
  for each w in words
    freq[w] = freq.get(w,0) + 1
  return word with maximum freq (ties: any)
```
What is MOST-FREQ-WORD('the cat and the dog the')?

Pseudocode:
```
REMOVE-DUP-WORDS(S)
  words = parse words from S
  seen = empty set
  res = empty list
  for w in words
    if w not in seen
      append w to res
      add w to seen
  return join(res,' ')
```
What is REMOVE-DUP-WORDS('this is is a test test')?

Pseudocode:
```
ATOI(S)
  i = 1; sign = 1
  if S[i] == '-' then sign = -1; i = i + 1
  else if S[i] == '+' then i = i + 1
  val = 0
  while i <= length[S]
    val = val * 10 + (ord(S[i]) - ord('0'))
    i = i + 1
  return sign * val
```
What is ATOI('-42')?

Pseudocode:
```
ITOA(n)
  if n == 0 return "0"
  sign = ''
  if n < 0 then sign = '-'; n = -n
  digits = empty list
  while n > 0
    append char('0' + (n % 10)) to digits
    n = n / 10
  reverse digits
  return sign + join(digits)
```
What is ITOA(-120)?

Pseudocode:
```
TRIM(S)
  i = 1; while i <= length[S] and S[i] == ' ' do i = i + 1
  j = length[S]; while j >= 1 and S[j] == ' ' do j = j - 1
  if i > j return ""
  return S[i..j]
```
What is TRIM('  hello  ')?

Pseudocode:
```
REMOVE-MULTI-SPACES(S)
  res = ""
  i = 1
  while i <= length[S]
    if S[i] != ' ' then res = res + S[i]; i = i + 1
    else
      res = res + ' '
      while i <= length[S] and S[i] == ' ' do i = i + 1
  return res
```
What is REMOVE-MULTI-SPACES('a   b  c')?

Pseudocode:
```
REPLACE-CHAR(S, old, new)
  for i = 1 to length[S]
    if S[i] == old then S[i] = new
  return S
```
What is REPLACE-CHAR('banana', 'a', 'x')?


Pseudocode:
```
NEXT-PERMutation(S)

```

Pseudocode (KMP-based STRSTR, linear-time):
```
COMPUTE-LPS(P)
  // standard LPS computation
KMP-STRSTR(T,P)
  // uses LPS to scan T in O(n)
  return index of first occurrence or -1
```
What does KMP-STRSTR('abxabcabcaby', 'abcaby') return (1-based index)?

Pseudocode (strncmp: compare up to n characters):
```
STRNCMP(S1,S2,n)
  for i = 1 to n
    if S1[i] < S2[i] return -1
    if S1[i] > S2[i] return 1
    if S1[i] == '\0' and S2[i] == '\0' return 0
  return 0
```
What does STRNCMP('abcde','abczf', 3) return?

Pseudocode (strcpy: copy including terminating null):
```
STRCPY(dest, src)
  i = 1
  while src[i] != '\0'
    dest[i] = src[i]
    i = i + 1
  dest[i] = '\0'
  return dest
```
After STRCPY(dest, 'hello'), what is dest (as a string)?

Pseudocode (strncpy: copy at most n chars, no guarantee of null if n<=len):
```
STRNCPY(dest, src, n)
  i = 1
  while i <= n and src[i] != '\0'
    dest[i] = src[i]
    i = i + 1
  while i <= n
    dest[i] = '\0'
    i = i + 1
  return dest
```
What does STRNCPY(dest,'hello',3) produce as dest string (assume C-style null termination after 3 chars)?

Pseudocode (strcmp: lexicographic compare):
```
STRCMP(S1,S2)
  i = 1
  while S1[i] != '\0' and S2[i] != '\0'
    if S1[i] != S2[i] return (S1[i] < S2[i]) ? -1 : 1
    i = i + 1
  if S1[i] == S2[i] return 0
  return (S1[i] == '\0') ? -1 : 1
```
What does STRCMP('apple','apricot') return (as -1/0/1)?

Pseudocode (strrev: reverse in-place):
```
STRREV(S)
  i = 1; j = length[S]
  while i < j
    swap S[i], S[j]
    i = i + 1; j = j - 1
  return S
```
What is STRREV('stressed')?

Pseudocode (strncat: append at most n chars from src):
```
STRNCAT(dest, src, n)
  i = length[dest]
  j = 1
  while j <= n and src[j] != '\0'
    dest[i+1] = src[j]
    i = i + 1; j = j + 1
  dest[i+1] = '\0'
  return dest
```
If dest initially contains 'Hello' and src='World', what is dest after STRNCAT(dest, src, 3)?

Pseudocode (tokenize with strtok-like behavior):
```
STRTOK(S, delim)
  // successive calls return next token, consecutive delims are skipped
```
Given S = 'one,two,,three' and delim = ',', the successive tokens returned are:

Pseudocode (recursive strstr):
```
RECURSIVE-STRSTR(T,P)
  if length[P] == 0 return 1
  if length[T] < length[P] return -1
  if T[1..length[P]] == P return 1
  sub = RECURSIVE-STRSTR(T[2..end], P)
  return (sub == -1) ? -1 : sub + 1
```
What does RECURSIVE-STRSTR('hello world','world') return (1-based index)?

Pseudocode (strstr implemented without explicit loops using recursion):
```
STRSTR-NOLOOP(T,P)
  // recursively slide pattern over text
```
What is STRSTR-NOLOOP('aaaa','aa') (1-based index)?

Pseudocode (remove at most one char to test palindrome):
```
CAN-BE-PAL-ONE-REMOVE(S)
  i = 1; j = length[S]
  while i < j
    if S[i] == S[j] then i++, j--
    else return IS-PAL(S[i+1..j]) or IS-PAL(S[i..j-1])
  return TRUE
```
Does CAN-BE-PAL-ONE-REMOVE('abca') return TRUE or FALSE?

Pseudocode (can rearrange into palindrome check):
```
CAN-REARRANGE-PAL(S)
  freq = count chars
  odd = number of chars with odd freq
  if length[S] is even return odd == 0
  else return odd == 1
```
Can 'carrace' be rearranged into a palindrome?

Pseudocode (count palindrome partitions via backtracking):
```
COUNT-PAL-PARTS(S)
  // count number of ways to partition S into palindromic substrings
```
How many palindrome partitions does 'aab' have? (partitions listed: ['a','a','b'], ['aa','b'])

Pseudocode (min-cut DP for palindrome partition):
```
MIN-CUT-PAL(S)
  // compute min cuts so each partition is palindrome
```
What is MIN-CUT-PAL('aab')?

Pseudocode (LPS via DP for longest palindromic subsequence):
```
LPS(S)
  // DP computing length of longest palindromic subsequence
```

Pseudocode (count palindrome subsequences brute-force for small strings):
```
COUNT-PAL-SUBSEQ(S)
  // enumerate subsequences and count those that are palindrome
```
How many palindromic subsequences (including duplicates from different indices) does 'aaa' have?

Pseudocode (find palindrome pairs among words):
```
PALINDROME-PAIRS(WORDS)

```
Given ['bat','tab','cat'], how many palindrome pairs exist?

Pseudocode (shortest palindrome by prefix matching & reverse):
```
SHORTEST-PAL(S)

```


```
LONGEST-PAL-SUFFIX(S)

```

Pseudocode:
```
HAS-PATH-SUM(node, sum)
  if node==NULL return FALSE
  if node.left==NULL and node.right==NULL return node.val==sum
  return HAS-PATH-SUM(node.left, sum-node.val) or HAS-PATH-SUM(node.right, sum-node.val)
// TREE = [5,4,8,11,null,13,4,7,2,null,null,null,1]
```

```
  while curr or stack not empty

      push curr; curr=curr.left
    curr = pop; visit curr; curr = curr.right
```

Pseudocode:
```
TREE = [1,2,3,4,5,6,7]
// Level-order (BFS) using queue
BFS(root)
  enq(root)
  while queue not empty
    node = deq(); visit(node)
    if node.left enq(node.left)
    if node.right enq(node.right)
```
